I focused on the only candidate that the prior log has not refuted: the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9‚Äì33. The prompt fixes the presentation format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma = tau^2, pro-2-core generators after them, and exponents modulo M=85667662080.The prior file also makes clear that the earlier scalar, plain-œÄ2, and fixed-œÑ‚àí2 candidates were killed by finite tame or index-two subgroup tests, so I did not treat them as live.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with œÄ=œÄ2‚ààZ and Œ∑=2œÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xœÉ‚àíœÄxœÉœÄx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ ((yœÑ)œÄ)œÉy‚àí1,where œÄ‚â°40491355905,‚àíœÄ‚â°45176306175,‚àíŒ∑‚â°50932630783(modM).The literature context supports the prior log‚Äôs framing: the known Jannsen‚ÄìWingberg-style local-field presentation method is formation-theoretic over finite tame subextensions, and not just a check of abstract Demu≈°kin kernels; Neukirch‚Äôs report describes that explicit determination program, while Jarden‚ÄìShusterman record finite presentability and generator/relation counts for local absolute Galois groups.Numdam+1 Labute‚Äôs classification and the dyadic orientation invariant are exactly the invariant package the prior turns have been testing.Numdam+1 My main finding is that Turn 33‚Äôs two ‚Äúcorrections‚Äù are themselves reversed. The conclusions it wanted are mostly still salvageable, but the displayed fractions must be fixed before any proof can be trusted.First, in the sign-cover calculation, let V=kerŒ∫ be the sign double cover of a finite-tame pro-2 kernel. Let t be the deck involution, and work on a nontrivial tame-inertia character block where œÑ‚Ü¶Œ±ÓÄÝ=1. For the norm wordNa (u)=(uœÑa)œÄ,the Fox coefficient is the completed geometric sumDa=j=0‚àëœÄ‚àí1(tŒ±a)j.

The correct identity isDa=1‚àítŒ±a 1‚àí(tŒ±a)œÄ=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít,because œÄ‚â°1(mod2) and œÄ‚â°0(mode), so (tŒ±a)œÄ=t. Turn 33 wrote the reciprocal. That reciprocal is not harmless: 1‚àít is not a unit in the 2-adic sign-cover group algebra.With the corrected formula, the desired unit-pivot conclusion still follows. Since Œ±aÓÄÝ=1, the element 1‚àítŒ±a is a unit, while 1‚àít lies in the radical. Hence Da lies in the radical, and the coefficient contributed byx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ is ‚àí1+D‚àíŒ∑, a unit. Thus every nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block contributes only unit Smith pivots to the sign-kernel abelianization.Second, in the trivial tame-inertia block, the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚àís)X+(1+T)Y=0,s‚â°œÄ(modf),where T is the œÉ-cycle operator. If f=2a m with m odd, then s‚â°1(mod2a),s‚â°0(modm).

When f is even, s is odd, so 1+T‚à£1+T‚àís. The legitimate elimination is therefore

Y=P(T)X+YÀâ,P(T)=1+T1+T‚àís,not the reciprocal. With this P, the reduced multiplier is h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T‚àí1).

For a nontrivial f-th root Œ∂,(1+Œ∂)h(Œ∂)=Œ∂‚àís+Œ∂s+1.

So h(Œ∂)=0 would force Œ∂2s+1=1. But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), hence gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Therefore no nontrivial f-th root is a zero of h, and h(1)=1. The trivial block remains nonsingular, but only with the quotient in this direction.Using these corrected formulas, the finite-tame abelianization checks can be written cleanly. ForŒîe,f=‚ü®œÉÀâ,œÑÀâ‚à£œÑÀâe=1,œÉÀâf=1,œÉÀâœÑÀâœÉÀâ‚àí1=œÑÀâ2‚ü©,e¬Ýodd,e‚à£2f‚àí1,let Ue,f be the pro-2 kernel induced by the candidate. LetŒª=e‚àí1j=0‚àëe‚àí1œÑÀâj be the idempotent projecting to the tame-inertia-invariant part, and let S=œÉf be the marked unramified pro-2 generator. Abelianizing the orbit of the relation gives(œÉÀâ‚àís+Œª)X+(ŒªœÉÀâ‚àí1)Y=0 up to the harmless convention of whether the œÉ-shift is written as T or T‚àí1.On the (1‚àíŒª)-part, this relation solves Y in terms of X, so that block is torsion-free of rank (e‚àí1)f. On the Œª-part, it reduces to the cyclic unramified relation(1+T‚àís)X+(T‚àí1)Y=0.The T‚àí1 incidence block gives f‚àí1 unit Smith pivots. Summing around the cycle leaves the single relation2i=0‚àëf‚àí1Xi=0.

Thus the invariant block contributes Z2f‚äïC2, and S contributes one additional free generator. ThereforeUe,fab‚âÖZ2ef+1‚äïC2.

For the sign kernel Ve,f=kerŒ∫, the corrected nontrivial-character calculation above shows that nontrivial tame-inertia blocks still have only unit pivots. The only torsion comes from the œÑ-invariant unramified block. WritingœÉœÄ=œÉsSq,q=f1‚àís‚ààZ2,the nonunit Smith factor in that block is 2(fq+s+1).

For the candidate‚Äôs q=(1‚àís)/f, this is 2((1‚àís)+s+1)=4.

Hence the repaired sign-kernel conclusion isVe,fab‚âÖZ22ef+1‚äïC4.So the live candidate survives this audit, but Turn 33‚Äôs written formulas should not be reused as-is. The correct pair isDa=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít,P(T)=1+T1+T‚àís.This does not close the problem. The late prior turns are right about the remaining issue: matching abelianizations, cup products, sign kernels, and cyclotomic branch behavior does not by itself identify the full filtered (œÉ,œÑ)-operator action. The missing step is still a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem comparing the exact operator package selected by the candidate with arithmetic Frobenius and tame inertia over every finite tame subextension.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains unrefuted, but Turn 33 had two inverted fractions. The correct sign-cover coefficient isDa=(1‚àít)/(1‚àítŒ±a),and the correct trivial-block elimination is

P(T)=(1+T‚àís)/(1+T).

With those repairs, the uniform conclusionsUe,fab‚âÖZ2ef+1‚äïC2,(kerŒ∫)ab‚âÖZ22ef+1‚äïC4 still follow.

GAP: This is not yet a presentation proof. The remaining missing ingredient is the dyadic formation/rigidity theorem: the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered action, including œÉœÄ=œÉsS (1‚àís)/f and the dynamic tame correction œÑ‚àí2œÄ, must be identified with the arithmetic action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Use the corrected formulas as the stable algebraic base, then compare the exact marked operator package(Ue,f,œá,S,œÉœÄ,œÑ‚àí2œÄ)with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic in the first mixed tame cases (e,f)=(3,2) and (5,4) through U/U5, where filtered-action ambiguities first become visible.[BREAKTHROUGH]
